content.rendered:
On August 22, 1987, Bruce made a guest appearance with Levon Helm’s All -Stars at The Stone Pony on August 22, 1987, and in addition to a rocking cover of “Lucille,” they just had to play one of The Band’s greatest hits.

Take a listen:

And of course, here’s the original by The Band from way back in 1969.

Up on Cripple Creek
First performed: August 22, 1987 (Asbury Park, NJ)
Last performed: April 21, 2017 (Asbury Park, NJ)

© January 29, 2018

excerpt.rendered: Get (very) up close and personal as Bruce and Levon Helm’s All-Stars play “Up on Cripple Creek” at The Stone Pony on August 22, 1987.

jetpack-related-posts.title: August 22
jetpack-related-posts.author: Ken
jetpack-related-posts.excerpt: On this date: Bruce makes a guest appearance with Levon Helm, Little Steven reunites with the E Street Band, and the original Miami Horns make their final appearance on an E Street stage.

jetpack-related-posts.title: Cover Me: Ruby Baby
jetpack-related-posts.author: Ken
jetpack-related-posts.excerpt: Twice only: Bruce Springsteen and the E Street Band turn in steamy performances of "Ruby Baby" at The Stone Pony in the summer of 1987.

jetpack-related-posts.title: Come on Over to My Place
jetpack-related-posts.author: Ken
jetpack-related-posts.excerpt: The Drifters' classic makes frequent appearances at the Stone Pony in 1982.

jetpack-related-posts.title: Rock Baby, Rock It
jetpack-related-posts.author: Ken
jetpack-related-posts.excerpt: One time only: Bruce Springsteen joins Stone Pony house band Cats on a Smooth Surface to cover a lost rockabilly classic.

jetpack-related-posts.title: Funky Broadway
jetpack-related-posts.author: Ken
jetpack-related-posts.excerpt: One time only: Bruce joins short-lived Stone Pony house band The Shots for a cover of Wilson Pickett's hit, "Funky Broadway."

jetpack-related-posts.title: Cover Me: Come On, Let’s Go
jetpack-related-posts.author: Ken
jetpack-related-posts.excerpt: Bruce and Cats on a Smooth Surface cover Ritchie Valens' very first hit.
